title: India gold market update: Demand cools
gist: India's gold demand cooled in May-June 2026 as a mid-May import-duty hike from 6% to 15% and INR depreciation pushed domestic prices up 13% y-t-d, triggering record gold ETF outflows, a 39% m/m drop in imports, deep then narrowing domestic discounts, and a seasonal off-season lull amplified by the Prime Minister's appeal to curb buying.

insights.claim: As of 15 June 2026, international gold prices were broadly flat year-to-date while Indian domestic gold prices were up around 13.2%, largely reflecting a 9.0 percentage-point increase in import duty in mid-May and a 5.3% depreciation in the INR against the US dollar.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Domestic gold outperformance is policy- and FX-driven, not demand-driven; the gap is vulnerable to duty reversal or INR stabilization.
insights.quote: while international prices are broadly flat, domestic prices are up around 13.2%, largely reflecting the 9.0% increase in import duty in mid-May, and the 5.3% depreciation in the INR against the US dollar

insights.claim: The import duty on gold was raised from 6% to 15% on 13 May 2026, a key driver of the cooling in Indian gold demand and imports.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A 9pp duty hike is a structural demand dampener for the world's second-largest gold market; watch for smuggling and grey-market substitution.
insights.quote: Import duty was raised from 6% to 15% on 13 May 2026.

insights.claim: Elevated inflation concerns have raised expectations that major central banks will tighten monetary policy, increasing the opportunity cost of holding gold and pressuring its recent performance.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Macro headwind for gold: rate-tightening expectations and improved risk sentiment cut investment demand globally, not just in India.
insights.quote: this has raised the opportunity cost of holding gold and pressured its recent performance

insights.claim: After the mid-May duty hike, the Indian domestic gold discount to landed price widened from an average of US$14/oz to nearly US$150/oz, driven by profit-taking, offloading by dealers who imported pre-hike, and old-jewellery inflows, before narrowing to around US$25/oz by 15 June.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: The discount spike and rapid normalization signal a demand-supply imbalance that has largely cleared; a real-time gauge of Indian physical demand health.
insights.quote: the gap widening from an average of US$14/oz before the hike to nearly US$150/oz after

insights.claim: Indian gold jewellery and bar/coin demand stayed subdued through May and early June, hurt by a seasonally soft period, an inauspicious Hindu calendar period (Adhik Maas, 15 May to 15 June), price volatility prompting wait-and-watch behavior, and new store openings slowing.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Multiple overlapping drags on Indian retail gold demand; participants expect softness through June-July with recovery from August seasonal cycle.
insights.quote: gold jewellery demand remained subdued through May and early June, a seasonally soft period that was further affected this year by an inauspicious period as per the Hindu calendar

insights.claim: The Prime Minister's appeal to limit gold buying weighed on discretionary purchases, especially in urban markets, and retailers report old-gold exchange business rising 5-15% and accounting for as much as 60-70% of sales for some.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Political pressure plus duty is suppressing fresh gold purchases and shifting volume to recycled gold, reducing net import demand.
insights.quote: the Prime Minister's appeal to limit gold buying weighed on discretionary purchases, particularly in urban markets

themes.theme: Investment flows: ETFs and digital gold

insights.claim: Indian gold ETFs recorded their first monthly net outflow since April 2025 in May 2026, with net outflows of INR7.25bn (US$76mn), the largest on record in rupee terms, and a record 134,343 decline in investor folios, before flows turned positive in early June with INR16.31bn (US$171mn) of net inflows during 1-11 June.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Record ETF outflows reflect profit-taking on the duty-driven price spike, not structural exit; the swift June reversal signals demand resilience.
insights.quote: net outflows stood at INR7.25bn (US$76mn), the largest on record in rupee terms
insights.locator: Gold ETFs: flows reverse in May

insights.claim: Several Indian fund houses introduced temporary caps on large gold ETF investments (direct subscriptions capped at INR25cr/~US$2.6mn and fund-of-funds lump-sums at INR10 lakh/~US$10.6k per PAN per month) amid concerns over imports, external balances, and currency pressures.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Investment caps could throttle institutional gold ETF inflows, with corporates holding 58% of AUM; a policy-linked constraint on the demand channel.
insights.quote: with direct subscriptions capped at INR25cr/~US$2.6mn

insights.claim: Digital gold purchases via UPI moderated in May, with transaction values down 2% m/m to INR24bn (US$256mn) and volumes down 5% m/m to 1.54t, yet volumes stayed above the 16-month average of 1.36t and 38-41% below the January peak.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Digital gold buying is cooling but holding above trend, indicating underlying retail interest persists despite the price and policy backdrop.
insights.quote: volumes remained above the 16month average6 of 1.36t, suggesting that buying interest remains relatively strong despite the moderation
insights.locator: Digital gold buying slows, interest holds

insights.claim: Against the mid-May duty increase, Indian gold imports fell 39% m/m to US$3.4bn (estimated 25-30t versus April's 46t and a two-year average of 59t), though they remained 34% higher y/y, and gold's share of total merchandise imports dropped to about 5% from an elevated ~14% in January-February.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Sharp import contraction eases India's external-balance and currency pressure, the likely policy intent behind the duty hike; a bearish signal for near-term physical gold demand.
insights.quote: gold imports declined 39% m/m to US$3.4bn, though they remained 34% higher y/y
